name: Vladimir Putin Elected President of Russia
summary: Vladimir Putin won his first presidential election, marking the beginning of a new political era in Russia. His leadership would reshape Russian domestic and foreign policy for decades.

name: Concorde Air France Flight 4590 Crash
summary: A Concorde jet crashed shortly after takeoff from Paris, killing all onboard and several on the ground. The accident led to safety concerns and contributed to Concorde's retirement.

name: Sydney Summer Olympics
summary: Sydney hosted the 2000 Summer Olympics, widely regarded as one of the best-organized Games. The event highlighted Australia's culture and athletic excellence on the world stage.

name: Millennium Summit of the United Nations
summary: World leaders gathered for the largest summit in UN history to address global poverty, conflict, and development. The conference produced the Millennium Development Goals.

name: Ehime Maru-USS Greeneville Collision
summary: A U.S. submarine struck and sank a Japanese training vessel during an emergency surfacing drill. The event strained U.S.–Japan relations and led to changes in naval procedures.

name: 2000 United States Presidential Election
summary: The election between George W. Bush and Al Gore became one of the most disputed in U.S. history. A Supreme Court ruling ultimately resolved recount disputes in Florida.

name: Second Intifada Begins
summary: Following a visit by Ariel Sharon to the Temple Mount, widespread unrest erupted across Palestinian territories. The violence dramatically affected Israeli-Palestinian relations.

name: Dot-Com Bubble Peaks
summary: Technology stock valuations reached their highest levels at the height of the dot-com boom. The bubble soon burst, leading to widespread financial losses.

name: 'I Love You' Computer Virus Outbreak
summary: The 'I Love You' worm spread rapidly through email systems worldwide, infecting millions of computers. It caused billions in damages and exposed major cybersecurity vulnerabilities.

name: Kim Dae-jung Awarded Nobel Peace Prize
summary: South Korean President Kim Dae-jung was honored for promoting democracy and pursuing peace with North Korea. His Sunshine Policy aimed to reduce tensions on the Korean Peninsula.

name: Sierra Leone Civil War Ceasefire
summary: A ceasefire agreement brought hope for an end to Sierra Leone's brutal civil war. The deal helped pave the way for peacekeeping and reconstruction efforts.

name: ISS Receives First Long-Term Crew
summary: Expedition 1 docked with the International Space Station, beginning continuous human habitation in orbit. This marked a major milestone in global scientific cooperation.

name: Taba Summit Peace Negotiations
summary: Israeli and Palestinian negotiators met in Taba for intensive peace discussions. Though no final agreement was reached, the talks made substantial progress on core issues.

name: Portuguese Transfer of Macau to China Finalized Politically
summary: After the 1999 handover, China completed the first formal year of administering Macau as a Special Administrative Region. The event symbolized China's expanding governance over former colonial territories.
